channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [MAFATIND] Mafatlal Industries: SVP Marketing Gaurav Gupta Resigns
item.description:
Mafatlal Industries Ltd announced the resignation of Mr. Gaurav Gupta, Senior Vice President-Marketing. Mr. Gupta tendered his resignation citing personal reasons. The company accepted his resignation on April 12, 2026, with his last day of service being April 13, 2026. He will be relieved from his duties at the close of business hours on the effective date.

  • Gaurav Gupta, Senior Vice President-Marketing, has resigned.
  • Resignation was accepted on April 12, 2026.
  • His last day of service will be April 13, 2026.
  • Personal reasons were cited for the resignation.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: MAFATLAL INDUSTRIES LTD.-$ | Category: Management Change

item.title: [UNICHEMLAB] Unichem Labs US subsidiary recalls Buspirone Hydrochloride tablets
item.description:
Unichem Pharmaceuticals (USA), Inc., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Unichem Laboratories Ltd, has voluntarily recalled its Buspirone Hydrochloride Tablets, USP, 5 mg, 500 count bottles, distributed in the USA. The recall was initiated due to an out-of-specification (OOS) assay result identified at the 12-month long-term stability time point. The company has not received any reports of adverse events related to this recall to date and emphasizes its highest priority on ensuring patient safety and maintaining product quality throughout its manufacturing and supply chain processes.

  • Unichem's US subsidiary voluntarily recalled Buspirone Hydrochloride tablets.
  • Recall due to out-of-specification assay result at 12-month stability.
  • No adverse events reported related to the product recall to date.
  • Company prioritizes patient safety and product quality standards.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: UNICHEM LABORATORIES LTD. | Category: USFDA

item.title: [TEXRAIL] Texmaco Rail Bags ₹23.57 Cr Order from Hindalco
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Texmaco Rail & Engineering Ltd has secured a domestic order valued at ₹23.57 crores (excluding taxes) from Hindalco Industries Limited. The order entails the supply of one BTAP Rake and one Break Van. The company anticipates executing this order within five months from the date of the purchase order. The filing confirms that this transaction is not a related party deal, and no promoter or promoter group holds any interest in Hindalco Industries Limited.

  • Texmaco Rail secured a ₹23.57 Cr domestic order.
  • Order received from Hindalco Industries Limited.
  • Contract for supply of one BTAP Rake and one Break Van.
  • Order execution expected within five months from PO date.
  • Transaction is not a related party deal.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: TEXMACO RAIL & ENGINEERING LTD. | Category: Deals

item.title: [RCOM] RCOM: PMLA Adjudicating Authority confirms asset attachments
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Reliance Communications Limited (RCOM) announced that the Adjudicating Authority under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002 (PMLA) confirmed provisional attachment orders (PAOs) on April 10, 2026. These orders, previously disclosed in November 2025, relate to assets of RCOM and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, Campion Properties Limited (CPL) and Reliance Realty Limited (RRL). The confirmed attachments include a 3.7-acre land lease in New Delhi held by CPL, a 132.07-acre plot in Navi Mumbai held by RRL, and various properties held by RCOM and RRL across Bhubaneswar, Chennai, Pune, and Navi Mumbai, including the Dhirubhai Ambani Knowledge City infrastructure. The company noted that it is currently undergoing a corporate insolvency resolution process.

  • PMLA Adjudicating Authority confirmed provisional attachment orders.
  • Orders impact RCOM and its wholly-owned subsidiaries CPL and RRL.
  • Attached assets include prime properties in Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Pune.
  • Confirmation affects company assets during insolvency resolution.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: RELIANCE COMMUNICATIONS LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [JYOTICNC] Jyoti CNC Subsidiary Faces French Legal Probe, EUR 4M Accounts Seized
item.description:
Jyoti CNC Automation Ltd's wholly-owned French subsidiary, Huron Graffenstaden SAS, is under investigation by French authorities regarding export controls and documentation of dual-use machinery. Interim measures include the temporary restriction of the subsidiary's director general, seizure of bank accounts totaling approximately EUR 4.0 million, and two residential properties owned by Jyoti SAS. A formal judicial investigation has been initiated. Huron Graffenstaden SAS refutes the allegations and plans to contest the actions. Jyoti CNC Automation believes the current business and operations of the subsidiary remain usual and expects no adverse impact on its standalone business, which contributes over 85% of the Group's revenue.

  • French authorities initiated a judicial investigation against Jyoti CNC's subsidiary, Huron Graffenstaden SAS.
  • Interim measures include restricting the subsidiary's DG and seizing ~EUR 4.0M bank accounts and properties.
  • Allegations involve violations of EU laws concerning dual-use technology exports.
  • The subsidiary refutes the allegations and is seeking legal advice to contest the actions.
  • Jyoti CNC expects no adverse impact on its standalone business, which generates over 85% of group revenue.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Jyoti CNC Automation Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [JYOTICNC] Jyoti CNC subsidiary faces French probe, EUR 4M accounts seized
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Jyoti CNC Automation's wholly-owned subsidiary, Huron Graffenstaden SAS, is under investigation by French authorities for alleged violations of export controls and documentation related to dual-use machinery. Interim measures include the temporary restriction of Huron Graffenstaden SAS's director general, the seizure of bank accounts totaling approximately EUR 4.0 million, and two residential properties owned by Jyoti SAS. A formal judicial investigation has been initiated. Huron Graffenstaden SAS refutes the allegations and plans to contest them. Jyoti CNC Automation believes the current business and operations of Huron Graffenstaden SAS remain usual and expects no adverse impact on its standalone business, which contributes over 85% of the group's revenue.

  • French authorities initiated investigation against Jyoti CNC's subsidiary Huron Graffenstaden SAS.
  • Probe concerns alleged export control violations for dual-use machinery.
  • Interim measures include seizure of ~EUR 4.0 million bank accounts and two properties.
  • Subsidiary's director general temporarily restricted; formal judicial probe initiated.
  • Jyoti CNC expects no adverse impact on its standalone business, contributing over 85% revenue.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Jyoti CNC Automation Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [PSB] PSB: Director Shankar Lal Agarwal Retires
item.description:
Shankar Lal Agarwal, a Part-Time Non-Official Director on the Board of Punjab & Sind Bank, has retired upon the completion of his one-year tenure. His appointment was effective from April 11, 2025, as per a Government of India notification. The bank has informed the stock exchanges about this change in directorship, in compliance with Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Shankar Lal Agarwal retired from PSB's Board.
  • He served as Part-Time Non-Official Director.
  • His one-year tenure concluded as per notification.
  • The change is reported under SEBI Regulation 30.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: PUNJAB & SIND BANK | Category: Management Change

item.title: [JASH] Jash Eng FY26 Revenue Flat at ₹736 Cr; Projects FY27 Growth to ₹875 Cr
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Jash Engineering Ltd reported consolidated unaudited revenue of ₹736 Cr for FY25-26, showing no growth over FY24-25's ₹735 Cr and falling short of the revised projection of ₹775-800 Cr. This shortfall is attributed to US tariff issues and the Middle East war, which caused dispatch delays. The company projects a consolidated sales revenue target of ₹875 Cr for FY26-27, representing approximately 19% growth, factoring in potential global economic slowdowns. March 2026 saw an order intake of ₹57 Cr, contributing to a consolidated order book of ₹827 Cr as of April 1, 2026. The US tariff has been reduced to approximately 15% from 50%, and the company has initiated refund claims. Investment plans for manufacturing facilities in the USA and Saudi Arabia have been deferred due to current uncertainties.

  • FY25-26 revenue flat at ₹736 Cr, below projection due to US tariffs and Middle East war.
  • FY26-27 revenue projected at ₹875 Cr, targeting 19% growth despite global uncertainties.
  • Order book strong at ₹827 Cr as of April 1, 2026, with significant international contribution.
  • US tariff reduced to 15% from 50%; company initiated refund claims for excess charges.
  • Investment in US and Saudi Arabia manufacturing facilities deferred due to geopolitical risks.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Jash Engineering Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [MOTHERSON] Samvardhana Motherson: Croatian subsidiary SMRC-Croatia dissolved
item.description:
Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd announced the voluntary dissolution of its indirect wholly-owned subsidiary, SMRC Automotive Interior Modules Croatia d.o.o. (SMRC-Croatia). The Commercial Court of Zagreb dissolved SMRC-Croatia from its register, effective April 11, 2026. The dissolution was initiated by SMRC-Croatia due to a lack of foreseeable new business and significant annual operating costs. In FY 2024-25, SMRC-Croatia's turnover was Euro 256,362 and its net worth was €6,448. These figures represent a negligible 0.00% contribution to the consolidated revenue and net worth of Samvardhana Motherson International Limited.

  • Samvardhana Motherson's Croatian subsidiary, SMRC-Croatia, dissolved.
  • Dissolution effective April 11, 2026, due to no new business.
  • SMRC-Croatia's FY25 turnover was Euro 256,362, net worth €6,448.
  • Financial impact on consolidated revenue and net worth is negligible.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd | Category: Subsidiary

item.title: [STYLEBAAZA] Baazar Style Retail opens new Express Baazar store in Balasore, total 270
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Baazar Style Retail Ltd has announced the opening of a new Express Baazar store in Balasore, Odisha. This latest addition increases the company's total store count to 270 as of the current date. The disclosure was made in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, signaling the company's ongoing strategy for retail network expansion.

  • New Express Baazar store opened in Balasore, Odisha.
  • Total store count for Baazar Style Retail now stands at 270.
  • Reflects the company's continued retail network expansion.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Baazar Style Retail Ltd | Category: Expansion
